description_long: This is a ceiling-mounted electric fan with three elongated, flat blades radiating from a central cylindrical hub. The fan's body consists of a rounded motor housing connected to a vertical downrod, which attaches to a cup-shaped ceiling mount. The blades are evenly spaced and have a slightly tapered design, giving the fan a streamlined and functional appearance. The surface is smooth and appears to be made of painted or powder-coated metal, with no visible blade guard or external wiring. The overall design is minimalist and utilitarian, suitable for providing air circulation in large indoor or semi-outdoor urban spaces. The fan lacks any visible control panel or decorative elements, emphasizing practicality over ornamentation.
description: A minimalist ceiling-mounted electric fan with three flat, evenly spaced blades and a smooth, cylindrical central hub. The design is functional and streamlined, with a simple downrod and ceiling mount.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three blades symmetrically spread from the central hub and the full length of the downrod and ceiling mount.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the thickness of the blades and the cylindrical hub, with the downrod and ceiling mount visible from the side.
description_view_2: Direct side view displaying the linear alignment of two blades and the central hub, with the downrod and ceiling mount in profile.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the back of the hub and the blades' attachment points, as well as the ceiling mount from a different perspective.
attribute_blade_guard_design: No blade guard is present; the blades are fully exposed with no mesh, grille, or protective covering.
